rule: Only mark verified after the user-visible acceptance check passes and evidence is recorded.
parallel_rule: Use a separate worktree per task; parallelize only when dependencies are verified and file ownership does not overlap.
inherited_capabilities: Codex command and file lifecycle rendering
Read Only, Default, and Full Access permission modes
mouse interaction and terminal selection behavior
native Codex sessions, resume, fork, and compaction
ChatGPT authentication for the Codex runtime

features.title: Contained Codex-derived Elpis foundation
features.user_visible_behavior: Elpis launches from its own repository and retains Codex-quality tools, permissions, sessions, and TUI behavior without donor-clone access.
acceptance: Build and launch Elpis from a clean checkout.
Complete one authenticated command and file-change turn.
Prove no runtime donor-clone access.

features.title: Fast first usable frame
features.user_visible_behavior: Typing elpis shows a usable TUI in under one second; optional services may finish visibly in the background.
acceptance: Measure process start to first usable frame for cold and warm launches.
Compare normal launch with optional configured services disabled without persistently changing configuration.
Identify the measured blocking path.
Reach a median usable-frame time under one second or record a user-approved revised target.
evidence: User reports the previous installed launch near five seconds and archived prototype under one second.
Before correction, the old unified Python MCP imported in 1.64 seconds at 105 MB and exposed 21 tools.
The RAG-only MCP handshake completes in about 0.04 seconds at 13 MB and exposes only query_knowledge_base.
A profiled fresh process scheduled its first TUI frame in 0.049 seconds, and Masih confirmed the fresh launch is fast.

features.title: Completely remove approved unwanted features
features.user_visible_behavior: Elpis is smaller while every retained Codex capability continues to work; unwanted features are deleted rather than merely hidden.
approved_remove: experimental UI except Keep computer awake
approved_retain: skills using user and project skill catalogs
Keep computer awake in settings, off by default
shared token, session, context, and memory primitives required by retained behavior
approved_renames: stop to kill, with Ctrl+K only when composer is empty
acceptance: Delete each approved feature's enum, dispatch, dedicated UI, tests, snapshots, and unreachable residue where safe.
Keep shared foundations required by retained behavior.
Pass remote build, focused checks, and launch smoke after each bounded deletion.
Verify removed names no longer resolve and retained names still work.
evidence: dd36256 removes the superseded root TUI, old Python agent main, and obsolete Debian builder.
3e06042 restores a fast RAG-only MCP surface with exactly query_knowledge_base.
The following subtraction deletes the now-unreferenced Python memory, shell, web, speech, parser, and tool-execution modules and removes their declared packages and dependencies.
b135e7a changes Codex command discoverability and names but does not fully delete most implementations.
419384d removes startup-tip rendering and hides test-approval; residual Codex implementation still requires audit.

features.title: Receipts, expiry, and visible model context
features.user_visible_behavior: The user can see why each context item is present, its size and lifetime, and what replaces it when it expires.
acceptance: Compare the visible ledger with the next model request and find no undisclosed item.

features.title: Persistent goal, checkpoints, and exact or lean continuation
features.user_visible_behavior: Work resumes with its goal, decisions, changes, evidence, and next action without replaying irrelevant history.
acceptance: Resume one task exactly and one task leanly, preserving required state in both cases.

features.title: Curated cross-session memory
features.user_visible_behavior: Relevant durable knowledge is retrieved with provenance and stale knowledge can be reviewed or removed.
acceptance: Recall a taught project fact in a related new session and omit it from an unrelated session.

features.title: Transparent /auto model routing
features.user_visible_behavior: Selecting /auto routes each task to an appropriate model from the user's pool and shows the choice and reason.
acceptance: Route agreed easy, medium, and hard examples to their configured tiers while allowing manual override.

features.title: Enforced creator and project behavior
features.user_visible_behavior: Applicable creator and project rules reach every runtime, with enforceable rules checked by code.
acceptance: Run the same behavioral evaluation against two runtimes and record pass or fail evidence.

features.title: Elpis visual identity without content changes
features.user_visible_behavior: Elpis changes colors and styling only while retaining every Codex information surface, layout behavior, and interaction.
acceptance: Compare representative Codex and Elpis transcripts and find no lost information or interaction.
Confirm the change is appearance-only.

features.title: Voice dictation into the composer
features.user_visible_behavior: The user can deliberately dictate text, review and edit the transcript in the composer, and choose when to submit it.
acceptance: Audit the contained Codex source for an existing dictation path before designing a new one.
Require visible recording consent and status.
Insert editable text without auto-submitting.
Handle unavailable microphone or transcription service clearly.

features.title: Gemini, Claude, and other runtime adapters
features.user_visible_behavior: A non-Codex runtime enters Elpis while Elpis retains its context, memory, policy, and evidence.
acceptance: Complete and resume one visible task through each supported runtime.
evidence: The experimental Gemini ACP seam is preserved as a named historical tip inside archive/pre-cleanup-20260716 and is not merged; this is intentionally low priority.

features.title: Installable and shippable product
features.user_visible_behavior: A new user can install, authenticate, launch, and complete a first task from a clean machine.
acceptance: Complete a clean-environment install and first-task smoke without repository-specific paths.
